trace.final_explanation: openclaw_per_ticker: 30/30 tickers produced a view via openclaw.

AAPL.final_explanation: AAPL valuation has disconnected from fundamentals — 50% PE expansion in 12 months on declining earnings creates unfavorable risk-adjusted reward.

AMGN.final_explanation: AMGN's P/E compression to ~15x (from 24x a year ago) creates a meaningful valuation entry point for a biotech with accelerating earnings, though FTC risk on the Horizon deal and massive debt keep conviction moderate.

CAT.final_explanation: CAT is a quality cyclical with strong momentum, but the P/E re-rating from 13x to 18x is complete and the stock now trades at fair value with limited asymmetric upside.

CVX.final_explanation: Energy sector earnings cycle has turned from 2022's windfall; CVX's trailing valuation is misleading and forward multiples are fair-to-full with crude at $75 and declining.

HD.final_explanation: HD faces a cyclical earnings downgrade amid frozen housing markets and post-pandemic demand normalization, yet valuation has not adequately repriced the risk.

INTC.final_explanation: INTC fundamentals continue to deteriorate with negative operating margins and no near-term earnings recovery catalyst; AI narrative is hope without evidence.

KO.final_explanation: KO is a quality business trapped in a premium valuation that the rate environment and margin trajectory don't support; the aspartame WHO risk adds a fresh headwind to an already unfavorable risk/reward.

MCD.final_explanation: MCD trades at a premium ~32x P/E near all-time highs with margin compression and flat revenue, offering limited upside against valuation risk.

MRK.final_explanation: MRK's valuation re-rating from deep-value (13x) to premium (22.6x) has overshot fundamentals — Q1 margin compression and the Keytruda patent cliff make risk-reward unfavorable at current levels.

PG.final_explanation: PG is a quality defensive hold at fair price — margin recovery is complete, valuation is full, and no catalyst exists to create an edge in either direction.

UNH.final_explanation: UNH remains the best risk/reward in managed care — valuation compression has overshot while fundamentals compound, and the June utilization warning is temporary normalization, not a structural change.
